  5. How Much Does It Cost to Register a Trademark in China?
Guide June 9, 2026 · 10 min read

How Much Does It Cost to Register a Trademark in China?

Registering a trademark in China costs between ¥300-¥3,000+ (approximately - USD), depending on the number of classes, complexity, and whether you use a professional agent.

💰

Key Takeaway

Registering a trademark in China costs between ¥300-¥3,000+ (approximately - USD), depending on the number of classes, complexity, and whether you use a professional agent. Understanding the complete cost structure helps you budget effectively and avoid unexpected expenses.

📋 Complete Cost Breakdown

Official CNIPA Fees

Basic Filing Fee:

  • ¥300 per class (up to 10 items)
  • ¥30 per additional item (over 10)
  • ¥150 priority claim fee

Examples:

Single class, 10 items: ¥300
Single class, 15 items: ¥450
Two classes, 10 items each: ¥600

Professional Agent Fees

RTMCN's Pricing:

  • ¥1,500 standard service per class
  • ¥2,500 premium service per class
  • ✓ Comprehensive search included
  • ✓ Full application management
  • ✓ No hidden fees

Total Cost Examples:

Single class (RTMCN): ¥1,800 ()
Two classes (RTMCN): ¥3,600 ()
Three classes (RTMCN): ¥5,400 ()

⚠️ Hidden Costs to Watch Out For

🎯

Classification Errors

Wrong class selection can cost ¥300+ to refile plus 3-6 months delay

📝

Objection Responses

CNIPA concerns can cost ¥1,000-3,000 to respond

⚖️

Opposition Defense

Third-party challenges can cost ¥3,000-10,000 to defend

🔄

Renewal Fees

Every 10 years, ¥500-750 plus agent fees

⚖️ Cost Comparison: DIY vs. Professional

Aspect DIY Approach Professional (RTMCN)
Official Fees ¥300 per class ¥300 per class
Service Fees ¥0 (your time) ¥1,500 per class
Translation ¥200-500 Included
Success Rate 60% 100%
Time Investment 20+ hours Minimal
Risk Level High Low
